rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF VARTKES ANIVIAN
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 1/14/25. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) No personal representative has been appointed yet for the estate of Alice Anivian. (2) Notice of Hearing still refers to "said ACCOUNTING PETITION," when there is NO ACCOUNTING. (3) Now, the third "supplement" filed on 11/18/24 has AMENDED the proposed distribution from the two schools to one school. An amending pleading (regardless of how it is captioned) requires notice. [CRC 7.53(a).] Please provide courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 12/31/24. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF JENNY LEA BROWNSON
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #3, Report
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 3/5/25. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) No proposed order has been lodged or received by the assigned examiner. (2) No proof of service of notice has been filed. (3) No bank statements have been filed. (4) The petition does not appear to address what the conservator proposes to do with the remaining assets. (5) The accounting does not appear to be for the period ending on the date of death and also a separate accounting for the period subsequent to the date of death. [Probate Code Section 2620(b).) (6) In any event, the second accounting has not yet been adjudicated, so a third accounting is premature. (The second could have been amended to include the post-death accounting period...) Please provide a proposed order and courtesy copies of the petition and any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 2/24/25. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MARGARITA VILLALTA-MOLINA
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 1/22/25. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) When an accounting is presented, original bank or brokerage statements must be filed to verify the ending balance(s) shown in the accounting. None appears to have been filed. [LRSF 14.34 D.] See CRC 7.575(b) for computer-generated printouts. (2) Exhibit B needs more information: as with the accounting, it should show the date, payee, description, and amount. What documents were sent? What "non-probate attorneys' fees" were paid (without a court order)? What was e-filed? The probate referee's commission was $75, but the executor paid him $75 and another $100? (3) What does the executor intend to do should any other assets come into the estate (after payment of the DHCS claim in full)? Deadline is 1/8/25 for providing the assigned examiner courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (NB: In order for this Court to track closed matters, orders to terminate a proceeding must begin with the following language: "Order for Final Distribution." [LRSF 14.35 I.6.b.] Please make a note of it.)

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF DOROTHY METLITSKI
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 1/7/2025. Petition appears to be an ancillary proceeding. Need a certified copy of the will to the clerk for a will file to be created. A certified copy of the order or decree admitting it to probate by the court (CT) must be filed separately and conventionally. The copy of the order (of COURT OF PROBATE, Hamden-Bethany Probate District) attached to the petition appears as a non-certified copy of the order for final distribution, not the order admitting the decedent's will to probate. Notice and publication do not set forth the information. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.
